def: adj. (in Gram.) Intransitive (verb).
adj. Useless, good-for-nothing, unprofitable.
s.m. Evil-doer, sinner, wretch.

ety: S. अङ्क, — rt. अञ्च्, — +करणीयं; — cf. also वक्र
def: v.n. To be or become crooked, twisted, contorted, distorted, contracted, &c., to be bent double with cramp, spasms, draw up the limbs in pain, to writhe; to become stiff, rigid, hard, &c.; to be frozen, paralyzed, &c.; — to be affected, pompous, vain, or conceited, to strut, swagger, move or go proudly, to bridle; to assume an arrogant or insolent air; to look defiantly (at), be ready to pick a quarrel (with, -se); to find fault (with), be displeased (at, -meṅ).
adj. Not to be done, improper to be done, unsuitable, unbecoming, inconsistent, indecent.
s.m. Attraction; fascination; a spasm; magnetic attraction; a magnet, loadstone.
adj. Pulling, drawing, attracting; attractive, magnetic; — s.m. Anything that attracts; magnet, loadstone.
s.m. Pulling, drawing, dragging, attracting, attraction; absorption (syn. kashish)
adj. Attractive, &c. = ākarshik.
adj. Attractive, magnetic ( = ākarshak).
part. Drawn, attracted.
s.m. = آکاس ākās, q.v.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit आकाश (ākāśa).

def: s.m. = آکاش ākāś, q.v.
s.m. = آکاش ākāś, q.v.

s.m. Space, vacuity; air, atmosphere; ether, the fifth element of the Hindūs (the subtle and ethereal fluid supposed to fill and pervade the universe, and to be the peculiar vehicle of life and of sound); sky, firmament, the heavens
subs: (S. ākāśa-vānī), s.f. A voice from heaven; revelation; oracle; divine call or injunction (syn. az-g̠aibī āwāz)
(S. ākāśa-vr̤itti), s.f. Uncertain or precarious source of subsistence; hand-to-mouth existence; — one who lives from hand to mouth, &c. ( = the next, q.v.)
adj. & s.m. Having no fixed source of subsistence; — one whose subsistence is fortuitous, one who lives from hand to mouth
ākās ćīẕ, s.f. Heavenly bodies (syn. ajrām)
(S. ākāśa-vallī), s.f. Air-creeper, a parasite, Cuscuta reflexa, or Cassyta filiformis (also called ākās-pavan)
s.f. The axis of the celestial sphere
s.f. The poles of the axis of the celestial sphere
ākās-dīyā (S. ākāśa-dīpa), s.m. A lamp lighted in honour of Lakshmī or Vishṇu, and elevated on a pole in the month of Kātik; a lantern on a pole; a beacon
s.m. The ethereal sphere; the celestial orb or globe
s.m. A devotee of the Śaiva sect (whose devotion consists in holding his face up to the sun, so that, after some time, it is difficult for him to hold it in the natural position)
s.m. The celestial sphere; the atmosphere
s.m. A plant that grows on the nīm-tree (a kind of epidendron), Bignonia suberosa

def: adj. = آکاسي ākāsī, q.v.
adj. Ethereal; atmospherical, aërial; celestial.
adv. Without a why or a wherefore; accidentally; immediately, on a sudden, suddenly, out of hand, unexpectedly, unawares, abruptly.
adj. Causeless, unforeseen, unexpected, sudden.
adj. Not to be uttered or mentioned, not to be spoken of; unspeakable, unutterable, inexpressible, indescribable; obscene.
adj. Not to be uttered or mentioned, not to be spoken of; unspeakable, unutterable, inexpressible, indescribable; obscene.
adj. Not to be uttered or mentioned, not to be spoken of; unspeakable, unutterable, inexpressible, indescribable; obscene.
s.m. = آك āk, q.v.
s.m. The straight piece of wood laid across the top of the framework on which the pulley of a well, &c. turns.
s.m. The city of Akbar, Agra.
adj. Relating to Akbar; — s.f. A kind of sweetmeat made of rice-flour and sugar, formed into balls, and after being fried in ghī, encrusted in clarified sugar
subs: A gold coin worth sixteen rupees.

def: s.m. A tie, knot; the marriage-knot, marriage ( = ʻaqd-ě-nikāḥ); a contract, compact, covenant, agreement, league, treaty, engagement; — responsibility, accountableness, suretiship; — (prob. corr. fr. A. ʻiqd) a necklace, a collar; a bulse, a string (of pearls, &c.)
subs: s.f. The tying of the marriage-knot; concluding a contract, &c.
v.t. To give (one) in marriage; to take in marriage, to marry, wed

def: s.m. Marriage-fees.
adj. superl. (from قدس 'to be holy'), Most holy or sacred; very holy, very sacred.
adj. (f. -ī), Single sole; solitary, lonely; — akelā, akele, adv. Alone, by ooneself, singly, unattended, unfriended; apart
subs: s.f. Statement or representation of one alone of the two parties concerned in a case

def: s.m. Loneliness, solitude; singleness, singularity; celibacy.
s.m. Loneliness, solitude; singleness, singularity; celibacy.
s.m. Brother
s.m. = آك āk, q.v.
s.m. Riddle, sieve; — one of a pair of grain-bags or water-bags used as panniers; bag, sack.
adj. Unfit for food, unlawful to be eaten, uneatable; — s.m. forbidden food.
= H اکهن अक्खन akkhan, (Braj.) s.f. pl. of āṅkh, 'eye.'
adj. Unbroken, whole, entire, all; indivisible, indestructible; — s.m. The Deity; — (in Math.), a point
adj. Not to be broken, or divided, indestructible, indivisible; irrefragable, irrefutable; incontestable, undeniable.
part. adj. Unbroken, continuous; unrefuted.
adj. Another, the other.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian آخِر (āxir), borrowed from Arabic آخِر (ʔāḵir).

def: s.f. Den of a wild beast ( = ākar, q.v.).
s.m. = اکشر akshar, q.v.
s.m. = اکهاڙا akhāṛā, q.v.
s.m. Arena, wrestling-ground, palæstra; place for exercise; any place of assembly; gymnasium; circus; theatre; court; — band, assembly, class; a sect of Hindū Sādhus (e.g. nārāyaṇī akhāṛā).
adj. Not small or short; large, long, tall.
v.n. To be disagreeable or displeasing; to be hard, difficult, heavy, burdensome, oppressive, or unsupportable.
s.m. Roughness, coarseness, rudeness, &c. (see akkhaṛ).
adj. (compar. & super. of خسيس ḵẖasīs), More, or most, avaricious; meaner; meanest; very mean or base.
adj. (compar. and super. of خاصّ), More especial; more, or most, excellent.
s.m. = اچهت aććhat, q.v.: — s.f. A portion of the crop per plough paid to the artisans of a village.
s.m. = اچهت aććhat, q.v.: — s.f. A portion of the crop per plough paid to the artisans of a village.
adj. Not liable to decay, undecaying, everlasting (see akshay)
subs: akhay-briksh, s.m. The immortal tree, — a species of Ficus famous in Hindū legend as undecaying

akhe-tīj (S. akshaya+tr̤itiyā), s.m. A festival observed on the third day of the bright half of Baisākh (April-May), which secures permanency to actions then performed. On that day the cultivators settle their accounts for the expenses of the Rabīʻ or spring crop, and the repayment of advances (also written akh-tij and akhā-tīj)
The rate of corn on the day of akhe-tīj, at which rate the cultivator agrees to repay in kind the advances made to him together with the interest thereon.

def: s.m.f. Taking, seizing; intercepting; seizure; exaction
subs: v.t. To take (e.g. baʻd aḵẖaẕ-i-naql); to adopt; to assume; to seize; to take away; to claim; extort; exact; to seize on, carp at.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic أَخْذ (ʔaḵḏ).

def: s.m. A writer of news; "our own correspondent"; editor (of a paper).
ex: وہ گھروں میں اخبار بانٹتا ہے۔
vō gharõ mẽ axbār bā̃ṭtā hai.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اَخْبَار (axbār), from Arabic أَخْبَار (ʔaḵbār).

def: adj. (compar. & super. of خبيث q.v.), Very impure or contemptible.
s.m. The chase, sporting, pursuing game, hunting; pursuing; terror, fright
subs: s.m. Beast of chase, wild animal

def: adj. Pertaining to the chase, for hunting (as a dog); s.m. Sportsman, hunter; hunting-dog.
adj. Hunting, chasing, pursuing; — s.m. A hunter; a hound.
adj. & s.m. Having small and weak eyes; suffering from nyctalopia (see din-aundhī); — One who has weak eyes, &c.
s.f. Live ashes; spark of fire.
s.m. Superintendence, supervision, government.
adj. All, whole, entire, complete, without a gap
subs: (akhila+īśvara), s.m. Lord of all, an epithet of the Deity (commonly applied to Rām, Śiva, &c.)

def: adj. The last; aftermost, final; latter, last-mentioned
subs: extremity; termination, issue; the latter part or portion (of)
āḵẖiruʼl-amr, āḵẖir-kār, āḵẖir-ko, adv. In the end; at last; after all; ultimately; eventually; once for all, finally
End of time; the latter times
The end of the (revenue) year; the last year
The latter part of the night; before dawn or break of day
The sixth month of the Moḥammadan year
v.t. To end, terminate, finish, conclude; to bring to an end, put an end to; to accomplish, execute
v.n. To come to an end, to be at an end, to be over; to come last; to cease to exist, to die.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian آخِر (āxir), borrowed from Arabic آخِر (ʔāḵir).

def: adj. & adv. = آخر āḵẖir, q.v.
subs: s.m. The last time, period or age; the last moment, the moment of death

def: adv. At last, ultimately, &c. = āḵẖir-ko, q.v.s.v. āḵẖir.
adv. At last, ultimately, &c. See āḵẖir, and āḵẖir-ko.
s.f. The other or next world, the world to come, the future state (contr. fr. dāruʼl-āḵẖirat or ḥayātuʼl-āḵẖirat)
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian آخِرَت (āxirat), from Arabic الْآخِرَة (al-ʔāḵira, “the hereafter”).

def: adj. Last, ultimate, &c. ( = āḵẖir, q.v.); of or belonging to the end; latter
subs: s.f. The latter part of the spring; the end of the harvest, or fruit season

s.m. The last Wednesday that falls annually in the month Safar, which is observed as a festival by the Moḥammadans of India with much solemnity and rejoicing, their prophet having found himself on that day well enough to walk abroad for the first time after a very severe illness
s.m. Adjustment of revenue at the end of the year.

def: s.m. Last or latest comers; posterity; — adj. Last
s.m. Little brother.
s.m. Morals, ethics; morality; virtues; good qualities; disposition; manners; politeness.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اَخْلَاق (axlāq), from Arabic أَخْلَاق (ʔaḵlāq), plural of خُلْق (ḵulq), خُلُق (ḵuluq). Compare Bengali আখলাক (akhlak).

def: s.m. The four humours of man (which were regarded by the ancients as the constituents of his composition), viz. blood, phlegm, choler (or yellow bile) and melancholy (black bile)
wety: Plural of خِلْط (xilt̤), borrowed from Classical Persian اخلاط (axlāt), itself from Arabic أَخْلَاْط (ʔaḵlāṭ).

def: s.m. A pulāʼo (q.v.) made of rice cooked in the juice of boiled meat, with fowl and spices.
s.f. Refuse, leavings; litter; sweepings; offals; filth; waste grass, &c. of stables
subs: s.f. Stuffing of litter or rubbish; a company or pack of low, vile fellows.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian آخور (āxwar).

def: s.m. The walnut tree and its fruit ( = aḵẖroṭ).
s.m. Understanding; wisdom; intelligence.
v.n. To be crooked, cross, rude, rough, contentious, &c. (with, -se)
s.m. Walnut; — the fruit of the Aleurites triloba.
s.m. Walnut ( = اخروٿ aḵẖroṭ, q.v.).
part. adj. Castrated, gelded; — s.m. A castrated animal; gelding
subs: v.n. To be castrated or gelded, &c.

def: s.m. A star; constellation; — good fortune, good omen; horoscope
subs: s.f. The calculation of one's horoscope.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اختر.

def: s.m. Bag and baggage, bed and bedding, goods and chattels, traps.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اختر.

def: s.m. Hawking and spitting; spit: — interj. Foh! pshaw! tush! pooh! fie!
s.m. = اکهے تيج akhe-tīj, q.v.s.v. akhay.
s.m. (f. āḵẖūn-nī), Teacher, tutor, preceptor, master
s.m. A kind of mineral; a loadstone.
s.f. = آخور āḵẖor, q.v.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian آخِر (āxir), borrowed from Arabic آخِر (ʔāḵir).

def: adj. Brotherly, fraternal; of or relating to a brother.
s.f. Name, appellation; renown, celebrity.
(pl. of خيف ḵẖaif), s.m. & f. Brothers who are sons, or sisters who are daughters, of one mother but of different fathers.
adj. By the same mother but by a different father (a brother or sister).
adj. (compar. & super. of خير), Better; best.
s.m. The good; the excellent; the better (people).
part. adj. Not famous, unknown, obscure; infamous.
part. adj. Counted, recited; said, spoken; declared, made known; called, denominated, named.
s.f. Infamy, bad repute.
adj. Green.
s.m. The heel; hinder part, rear (syn. pīćhā); — adv. or postpn. In the rear (of, -ke), in the wake (of); behind, after; in pursuit (of; — syn. pīćhe)
subs: (-kā), To go after, to follow, to pursue, &c.

ety: for A. عاقبة, quasi-inf. n. of عقب 'to succeed, follow,' &c.
def: s.f. End, termination, conclusion; futurity, the future; the future state or life; — adv. In the end, finally, ultimately, at last, after all
subs: The termination of the business, the long and short of the matter; — adv. Finally, &c. ( = ʻāqibat)
adj. Looking to the end; looking into futurity; far-seeing, far-sighted; provident, prescient, discreet, prudent, considerate
s.f. Regard to the end or issue or consequence, a looking to the future; far-sightedness; foresight, prudence
(-kī), To look to the future, to make provision (for), to provide (against); to exercise prudence
(apnī), To mar or to jeopardise (one's) future state of bliss
s.m. Provision for the next world; treasure in heaven; good works; bearing trouble or affliction with patience and resignation; — an infant gone to heaven.

ety: for A. عقيدة, v.n. fr. عقد 'to tie,' &c.; see ʻaqd
def: adj. Having firm belief or faith, being firmly persuaded; faithful, believing

ety: for A. عقيدة, v.n. fr. عقد 'to tie,' &c.; see ʻaqd
def: adj. Having firm belief or faith, being firmly persuaded; faithful, believing
s.m. A cornelian
subs: s.m. Mocha stone; — Indian shot, the seed of Canna indica.

ety: for A. عقيقة, s.f. fr. ʻaqīq, 'cleft,' &c.
def: s.m. The hair on the head of a new-born infant (so called because it is cut or shaved off on the sixth day); — the ceremony of shaving the head of an infant on the sixth day after childbirth, and a sacrifice made and a feast given on that day.

ety: act. part. of عقل 'to be or become intelligent,' &c.
def: adj. & s.m. Possessing intelligence or understanding, intelligent, wise, sensible; — a wise man, a sage.
(for A. عاقلة, fem. of ʻāqil), adj. f. = ʻāqil, q.v.

ety: ʻāqil, q.v.+Zend aff. āna or ana = S. अन
def: adj. & adv. Wise, intelligent; skilful (e.g. ʻāqilāna ʻilāj); — wisely, prudently, &c.

ety: ʻāqil, q.v.+ī = Zend aff i = S. इ
def: s.f. Intelligence, &c. ( = ʻaql).
adj. & adv. Only, sole, single; alone, singly.

ety: v.n. fr. عقم 'to be dry'; 'to be barren,' &c.
def: adj. Barren, having no children; that will not bear children; — ʻaqīma, s.f. A barren woman; a woman past child-bearing.

ety: for A. عقيمة, fem. of ʻaqīm
def: adj. Barren, having no children; that will not bear children; — ʻaqīma, s.f. A barren woman; a woman past child-bearing.
s.m. Poverty, destitution; labour, endeavour, exertion; — adj. Poor, needy, destitute, miserable, wretched.
s.m. Poverty, destitution; labour, endeavour, exertion; — adj. Poor, needy, destitute, miserable, wretched.
= آکنچن ākinćan, q.v.
s.f. Infamy, disgrace, obloquy, reproach; defamation, ill-report; bad repute.
s.m. Pellitory; pellitory of Spain, Anthemis pyrethrum.
s.f. Infamy, disgrace, obloquy, reproach; defamation, ill-report; bad repute.

ety: act. part. of عقّ 'to cut'; 'to be undutiful,' &c.
def: part. adj. Severing, cutting off (from); — undutiful, disobedient, refractory, rebellious
subs: v.t. To sever, cut off (from, -se), to disown; to disinherit

def: s.f. Sister (a respectful compellation for an elder sister, or any elderly female: cf. ākā).
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀏𑀓𑁆𑀓 (ekka), from Sanskrit एक (eka).

def: = T آکا ākā, s.m. Lit. 'elder brother' ( = آغا dg̠ā); brother; friend, dear friend; dear sir.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀏𑀓𑁆𑀓 (ekka), from Sanskrit एक (eka).

def: s.m. An enormous eater, a glutton.
ex: دیس میں بہت بھیانک اکال پڑا ہے۔
des mẽ bahut bhayānak akāl paṛā hai.
There is a terrible famine happening in the nation.
wety: From Sanskrit अकाल (akāla).

def: s.f. The Indian shot-plant, Canna indica (the name is peculiar to Southern India; in Bengal the plant is called sarva-jayā. The seeds are hard, black and shining, resembling shot, for which they are sometimes used).
s.m. Swallow-wort ( = آك āk,q.v.).
s.m. Riddle, sieve; — one of a pair of grain-bags or water-bags used as panniers; bag, sack.
adj. Half-blind, purblind; one-eyed.
= H آکهن आखन ākhan, (Braj.) s.f. pl. of āṅkh, 'eye.'
s.m. Plenty, abundance ( = آکر ākar, q.v.).

adj. Unformed, unfashioned, unpolished, uncivilized, undisciplined, rude, rough, coarse, barbarous, uncouth, awkward, clumsy; perverse, headstrong, wilful, stiff-necked, cross-grained, crooked; proud, haughty; contentious, quarrelsome; — s.m. A coarse, hard-hearted, or unfeeling person; an awkward or clumsy person, boor, clown, lout, dolt, &c. ( = ujaḍ, q.v.).
s.m. Roughness, coarseness, rudeness, &c. (see akkhaṛ).
s.m. Eating; food
subs: s.m. Eating and drinking, meat and drink.

ety: inf. n. of عقل 'to be or become intelligent,' &c.
def: s.f. Intelligence, wisdom, sense, understanding, intellect, mind, reason, knowledge; opinion
subs: (-kī), A veil to be drawn over the understanding (of), to be dull of understanding
To get sense, or intelligence; to have (one's) senses about one
or ʻaql ćakrānā, v.n. The head to be in a whirl; the brain to be a-wool-gathering; to be confused and distracted
v.n. The mind to be deranged; to be confused and distracted
or ʻaql dauṛānā, v.n. To use (one's) sense, to exercise (one's) mind, to consider, think, reason
or ʻaql-se dūr, adj. Inconceivable; unreasonable; nonsensical
s.m. Sole counsellor, mentor; — the angel Gabriel
v.n. To be bereft of sense or understanding, to lose (one's) senses, or wits
v.n. To get sense, to understand.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عَقْل ('aql), from Arabic عَقْل (ʕaql). First attested as Old Hindi अकलि (akali).

def: adv. Peevish; — fleet, swift.
adv. Reasonably; prudently; logically; by inference; by guess.
adj. Of or relating to intellect, &c.; intellectual, mental; rational, reasonable, judicious; deduced by reason; scientific.
s.f. Agitation; motion (esp. of a carriage).

ety: aql, q.v. + mand = Zend mañṭ = S. मन्त्
def: adj. & s.m. Possessed of intelligence, &c.; intelligent, wise, sensible; — a wise man, &c.
subs: (colloq.) 'A wise man's tail,' a fool, blockhead.
wety: From عَقْل ('aql, “intelligence”) + مَنْد (mand). First attested in c. 1635 as Middle Hindi عقلمند ('qlmnd).

def: s.f. Intelligence, wisdom, sound sense, sagacity, discretion; practical skill; cleverness, address; savoir faire; ingenuity, adroitness, craft
adj. Blind from birth; blind.
adj. compar. & superl. (of کامل kāmil), More, most, or very perfect or complete.
s.f. pl. (of کنف kanaf), Sides, parts, borders, confines, environs.
adv. Now, at present.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اَکْنُون (aknūn).

def: s.m. The plant Alangium hexapetalum, the oil of which is used in enchantments; — akolā (also), The plant Alangium decapetalum.
s.m. The top shoot of the sugar-cane ( = āk).
s.m. The plant Alangium hexapetalum, the oil of which is used in enchantments; — akolā (also), The plant Alangium decapetalum.
s.f. Bribe; lure (as, for example, is used to render tractable a cow that has lost her calf).
s.m. One who receives a bribe.
adj. (f. -ī), Dear, costly, high-priced

ety: S. अङ्कुरः; cf. H. अंकरी aṅkarī
def: s.m. A kind of vetch or grass which grows in the fields under the spring crop, twining round young corn and checking its growth, Vicia sativa: (it is used as fodder).
adj. (f. -ī), Stiff, rigid, cramped, frozen, paralyzed, &c.
s.m. A scorpion; the sign Scorpio; (met.) a quarrelsome person.
s.m. A near relative.
s.m. Fop, &c. = اکڙو akṛū, q.v.
s.m. Entering upon, beginning; ascending, superiority, surmounting, going over or beyond, surpassing; invading, invasion; a seizing.
s.m. pl. (of کرم karam), Favours, kindnesses, obligations; honour, respect.
adj. compar. & superl. (of کريم karīm), Most merciful; very kind, very generous, bountiful.
s.m. Entering; ascending; increase.
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit आक्रमण (ākramaṇa).

def: s.m. pl. (of قرين qarīn), Equals (in age, rank, &c.), contemporaries.
v.t. To distress; to obstruct, impede.
s.f. Ascending, rising.

ety: for اکڙي akṛī, from اکڙ akaṛ, q.v.
def: s.f. Excuse, plea, pretext, shift; — s.m. The funnel-shaped extremity of the bamboo tube attached to a plough; — uncleaned rice.
s.f. High price, dearness; scarcity, dearth bad season.
s.m. pl. (of قريب qarīb), Near relations, kindred, relatives, kinsfolk; friends; allies.
part. Drawn, attracted.
part. adj. Not done, not wrought, inartificial; not cultivated; wavering.
adj. Ungrateful.
s.f. Form, shape, figure ( = shakl), likeness, image; the body; species.
adj. Not made or wrought, inartificial, unfactitious, candid, genuine.
adj. Not made or wrought, inartificial, unfactitious, candid, genuine.
s.m. Walnut = اخروٿ aḵẖroṭ, q.v.
s.m. An affected person, fop, swaggerer ( = akaṛ-bāz, q.v.).
adj. Not cruel; gentle, mild, tender (syn. ḥalīm).
s.m. The reverse (of), the converse, or the contrary (of); counterpart; inversion; reflection (syn. parćhāʼīṅ), a shadow, a reflected image (as in a mirror, or water, &c.); — opposition; spite
subs: or ʻaks khaiṅćnā (-kā), To take the reflected image (of), to take a silhouette, or a photograph, &c.
(-ke), On the reverse (of); contrary (to), in opposition (to); — bar ʻaks is-ke, On the reverse (or the back) of this; — on the contrary.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عَکْس (aks), borrowed from Arabic عَكْس (ʕaks), from عَكَسَ (ʕakasa).

def: = A اقصي aqṣā, adj. superl. (of قصی qaṣī), Very remote; most distant; extreme; the extreme, extremely.
= A اقصا aqṣā, adj. superl. (of قصی qaṣī), Very remote; most distant; extreme; the extreme, extremely.
s.f. m. (?) pl. (of قسم qism), Sorts, kinds, various sorts, different kinds.
s.m. The greater number, the majority; — adv. Generally, for the most part, mostly, chiefly, often, frequently, commonly, usually
ex: ہَم اَکْثَر اِس مَحَلّے میں آیا کَرتے تھے۔
ham aksar is mahalle mẽ āyā karte the.
We used to come to this neighbourhood often.
اَکْثَر جو ہَم کَہْتے ہَیں وہ کَرْتے نَہِیں۔
aksar jo ham kahte ha͠i vo karte nahī̃.
We do not do what we usually say.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اَکْثَر (aksar), borrowed from Arabic أَكْثَر (ʔakṯar).

r: aks̤ara, aks̤araṉ, aks̤ar, aks̤arīya, aks̤arīyaṉ, aks̤arī
ety: for A. اکثرًا aks̤araṉ, the A. acc. of aks̤ar
def: adv. Generally, &c. = اکثر aks̤ar, q.v.

r: aks̤arīya, aks̤arīyaṉ, aks̤arī, aks̤ara, aks̤araṉ, aks̤ar
ety: for A. اکثريًا aks̤arīyaṉ, the A. acc. of aks̤arī , = P اکثره aks̤ara (for A. اکثرًا aks̤araṉ, theA. acc. of aks̤ar
def: adv. Generally, &c. = اکثر aks̤ar, q.v.
s.f. pl. (of قسط qist̤), Portions; instal ments.
s.m. A die for playing with (cubic or oblong); the spots on dice; terrestrial latitude
s.m. Letter (of the Alphabet); syllable
subs: s.m. A treatise on the alphabet.
ex: اِس شبد سے کچھ اکشر مٹ گئے ہیں
is śabd se kuch akṣar miṭ gae ha͠i
Some letters have been erased from this word.
اِس شبد کو اور اکشر جوڑو
is śabd ko aur akṣar joṛo
Add more letters/syllables to this word.
wety: From Sanskrit अक्षर (akṣara).

def: = اچهروتي aććhrauṭī, q.v.
adj. & s.m. Lettered, writing a good hand; — a lettered or scientific person; one who writes an elegant hand ( = aććharī).
adj. Infallible, remaining, abiding, indelible; — s.m. Whole grain; whole grains of rice used in religious ceremonies; fried grain (see aććhat)
subs: s.f. The ceremony of putting a few grains of rice on the forehead of an idol when addressed, or of a Brāhman when invited to an entertainment.

def: s.f. = اچهوهني aććhauhinī, q.v.
adj. Undecaying, imperishable; permanent, durable ( = aććhay)
subs: akshay-var (or bar), akshay vr̤iksh (or br̤iksh), s.m. A certain tree famous in Hindū legend as being undecaying (the tree in the under-ground temple in the fort of Allahabad is so termed).

def: s.f. Imperishableness; durability.
adj. & s.m. Unfortunate, unhappy, wretched, miserable; — an unfortunate wight, miserable person, &c.
s.m. Throwing away; convulsion; putting upon; reproof, censure, blame, reproach; irony, sarcasm; abuse.
s.m. The eye
subs: s.f. The pupil of the eye
s.m. A sign of the eye, a wink.

def: s.f. = اچهوهني aććhauhinī, q.v.
s.f. A certain tree = اکتي aktī, q.v.
adj. Reflected; reflex; obtained by reflection; photographic
subs: s.f. A reflected image, or picture; a silhouette; a photograph.

def: adj. Acting, officiating.
s.f. The Agaty tree, Sesbana (or Ӕschynomene) grandiflora: (also written agtī).
s.m. October.
wety: Borrowed from Portuguese outubro, influenced by English October. Compare Classical Persian اَکْتُوبَر (aktūbar), Konkani ओतुब्र (otubra), Marathi आक्तोबर (āktobar), all of which could be possible intermediaries.

def: adj. Without pedigree, relations, or family.

adj. (used chiefly as last member of compounds). Filled, full (of), overburdened (with); confounded, confused, agitated, flurried, perplexed, bewildered, disturbed, distressed, alarmed, anxious, restless, uneasy.
s.m. A great eater, a glutton ( = akkāl).
adj. Intelligent, wise, sagacious; rational.

v.n. To be confounded, confused, agitated, excited, distracted; to feel anxious, uneasy, disquieted, restless; to tire (of, -se), weary (of), be disgusted (with), be sick (of); to feel nausea or sickness.
s.f. Agitation, distress of mind, disturbance, distraction, anxiety, restlessness, uneasiness.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Of low origin or race, of mean extraction, low, base, mean, ignoble, plebeian; — a low-born person, &c.
adj. = اکلي akulī, q.v.
adj. = اکلي akulī, q.v.
adj. (f, of akuiī, q.v.), Bad, base (woman); — s.f. A woman of low caste or family, an ignoble or low woman.
part. adj. Agitated, distressed, &c. ( = ākul, q.v.).
s.m. Swallow-wort ( = آك āk,q.v.).
adj. Indestructible; keen, sharp.
adj. Unlucky, inauspicious.
s.m., = ? आकूति ākūti, s.f., Intention, purpose; wish, desire.
s.f., = S آکوت आकूत ākūt, s.m., Intention, purpose; wish, desire.
adj. Not afraid or threatened from any quarter, secure.
s.m. pl. (of قول qaul), Words, sayings, agreements, promises.
s.f. pl. (of قوم qaum), Tribes, peoples, nations; castes.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اقوام (aqvām), from Arabic أَقْوَام (ʔaqwām).

def: s.m. = آك īk, q.v.
s.m. = akwan, and āk, q.v.
s.f. Embrace, lap, bosom (also written aṅkwār)
subs: akwār lenā, To take to the bosom, to embrace, clasp ( = kaulī lenā).

def: s.f. Family name; patronymic.

ety: S. आर? but cf. S. alakta
def: s.f. Name of a tree from the root of which a red dye is prepared, Morinda citrifolia (the ordinary Sanskrit name of the tree is aćyut, which, in Hindī, is contracted to ać); — a pumpkin (cf. S. alābū); a green stalk of onion
subs: s.m. Colour extracted from the āl tree (called also ālī rang; see ālī).

def: s.f. Family, relations, kinsfolk; children, offspring, progeny, descendants; house, race, dynasty: (The restriction of this word to the maternal progeny, and that of aulād to the paternal, would not appear to exist in Arabic)
s.f. Damp, moisture, wetness.
intj. Mercy! quarter! God save us! Heaven preserve me!
subs: adv. Now; — al-ān ka mā kāna, adv. As before, as formerly, just as heretofore, as usual
adv. Decidedly, assuredly, certainly, of course, indeed, to be sure, precisely, truly; yes, very well; by all means
(for ʻalaʼt-tawakkul), adv. On trust, on the chance; by guess, conjedturally
adv. The result or consequence (is) in short, in fine; to sum up
adv. Now, at present; just now ( = abhī)
intj. God preserve me, or us! Heaven defend us!
adv. In truth, truly, verily, assuredly; actually, indeed; — intj. True! right! precisely! O yes! indeed!
s.f. The title of the first ṣūra of the Qorʼān
To read the first ṣūra of the Qorʼān previous to blowing upon a person's face in order to preserve him from evil influences
intj. God be praised! thank God!
(as euphonically for al), intj. Peace be on thee! safety attend thee! farewell! adieu!
s.m. Lit. 'the slave'; the signature or initials of a subordinate officer or servant
intj. Great is God! wonderful! extraordinary! (used to express fear, surprise, denial, &c.)
adv. The short (of it is); in short, in fine, in a word, finally, upon the whole
s.m. Complaint, complaining, demanding justice; — intj. Alas! help!
s.m. The hidden, the secret, the invisible, the unknown, the mysterious; — al-g̠aib ʻindaʼl-lāh, The hidden or secret is with God; God knows what is hidden; God knows; God is omniscient
adv. In a word, in short, &c. ( = al-g̠araẓ, q.v.)
s.m. The double; adj. Double, twice; twofold
intj. God be praised! &c. ( = al-ʻaz̤amatu liʼl-lāh, q.v.)
s.m. The last Friday in the month of Ramaẓān; — intj. Adieu! farewell! good-bye!

def: = اعلي aʻlā, q.v.
adj. (f. -ī), Wet, damp moist, saturated (especially with rain); running (as a sore).
s.m. A small recess in a pillar or wall for holding a lamp, &c.; niche; receptacle.
s.m. Basin for water round the foot of a tree.
s.m. The name of a certain Hindū hero and poet (from whom a species of poetry takes its name, — also written āihā); a song about Álā and Udal; a ballad; a heroic poem
subs: (apnā), To tell one's own story; to blow one's own trumpet; to tell a long story (of, -kā), to dwell on a subject.

def: s.m. Instrument, &c. = ālat, q.v.; (in Gram.) the instrumental case

ety: inf. n. of علي 'to be exalted in dignity, &c.'
def: s.m. Exaltation, superiority, sublimity, glory; — adj. Very exalted, most high or glorious.

ety: orig. a subst. fr. علو 'to be high,' &c.; and = علا ʻalā, q.v.
def: prep. On, upon, above; according to, &c. (syn. bar: it occurs only in Arabic phrases; and its final vowel is shortened in pronunciation before the Ar. article al)
subs: adv. (ʻalā+al+it˚) Continually, without intermission; uninterruptedly; consecutively, in succession, in a series, seriatim
adv. Continually, incessantly, perpetually; in perpetuity, permanently; assiduously, persevering
(i.e. ʻalā+al+taḥqīq), According to truth, in truth, verily, surely, certainly, of a certainty
At full length, in detail; prolixly, diffusedly
According to (its) vis-à-vis; (each) according to (its) kind or like
On trust, in faith; in blind confidence; hap-hazard
On account; — without a proper account, without keeping, or making up, an account; — s m. A suspense account
Continually, incessantly, perpetually, always; assiduously; in perpetuity, permanently, for ever
Against the will (of, -ke), in spite (of), in opposition (to)
or ʻalaʼṣ-ṣabāḥ, About dawn, towards daybreak, in the morning
Commonly, generally, in general, vulgarly (syn. ʻumūmaṉ)
(-ě), According to the power, or dignity, &c. (of)
On this measure, in the same manner, in like manner, similarly; and the like
On him; — ʻalai-hiʼr-raḥma, On him (be) the mercy (of God); — ʻalai-hiʼs-salām, On him (be) peace

def: adj. Of high rank or degree.
s.m. = āle-bāle, q.v.
s.f. = alāʼe-balāʼe, q.v.
s.m. Non-acquirement; loss: — adj. Unprofitable.
adj. Not to be attained, unattainable.
adj. = alaksh and alakh, q.q.v.
adj. & s.f. = alakshmī, q.v.
s.f. Pain, trouble, misfortune ( = alaiyā balaiyā); — adj. Wretched, miserable, unfortunate.
s.m. Fodder, provender (for beasts), food (for cattle), grass, hay, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عَلَف (alaf), from Arabic عَلَف (ʕalaf).

def: adj. Unattached, unconnected, disconnected, detached, separated, sundered; disjointed, dislocated; separate, distinct, loose; lonely, solitary; excluded, shut out; liberated, disengaged; free, independent; singular, standing alone, unique; peculiar, strange; placed apart or aside, (hence) secure, safe; — adv. Apart, aloof, asunder; adrift, alone; at a distance, far off or away; at liberty; imperceptibly, unperceived, unseen; — intj. Stand off! away! be gone!
subs: adj. emphat. of alag, q.v. Holding aloof; dissatisfied, displeased; — adv. Separately, one by one, severally, individually
adj. Fine, delicate, soft; — adv. Softly, gently, tenderly
(-se), To live away (from); to live apart, live alone; — to hold aloof, refrain (from), keep one's distance; to avoid; to keep out of the way

v.t. To sunder separate, sever, detach, disconnect; to displace, disjoint, dislocate; to set free, extricate, set at liberty, unfasten, unbind; to set apart; to part with, get rid of; to remove; to put away, divorce; to put aside; to exclude; to dispose of; to discriminate; to sift; to put out of the way; to embezzle; to put out of sight; to have done with, to close, wind up (a business, &c.); to give up, relinquish, abandon

v.n. To be separated, severed, &c. (see alag); to part, separate, go asunder; to be divided or partitioned; to be removed; to be got rid of, parted with, disposed of, to be dismissed; to be sifted, picked, selected; to be made away with, be embezzled, carried off, stolen; to become aloof, to back out of, to evade; to withdraw, retire; to slink off, away; to escape, get clean away.
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit अलग्न (alagna).

def: v.n. To be separated, &c. = alag honā, q.v.s.v. alag.
s.f. A rope, or a bamboo, stretched across a room or a yard, to hang clothes on; a clothes-line. (See arganī).
adj. & adv. = عليحده ʻalāḥida, q.v.
corr. of عليحده ʻalāḥida, q.v.

ety: A. علي ʻalā+حدة ḥidat, 'being alone,' &c., inf. n. of وحد
def: adj. & adv. Separate; distinct; — apart (from, -se); — separately; one by one; apiece
subs: v.t. To set apart; to appropriate
adv. Separately; one by one; apiece
v.t. To separate; to part with, to get rid of (syn. alag karnā)
v.n. To be separated (from, -se); to separate oneself (from), &c. ( = alag honā, q.v.).

ety: ʻalāḥida, q.v.+ī = Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. The state or condition of being separate or apart; separation; severalty.
see s.v. علی ʻalā.

ety: abst. s. fr. ālā, imperat. of ālūdan, see ālūda
def: s.f. Pollution, contamination; filth; purulent matter; garbage; — inconvenience, anxiety.

s.f. Misfortune, &c. = alāʼe-balāʼe, q.v.; (and as the women use the words, whilst waving platters with lamps, &c. round a person's head, to remove or avert all evil, they have come to signify Offering, sacrifice, victim); — lighted wisps with which the Hindūs divert themselves at the season of the Dewalī.
s.f. Lock of hair, curl, ringlet
subs: alkāval, alkavalī (S. alaka+āvalī), s.f. A row of side-curls.

def: adj. = alakh and alaksh, q.q.v.

ety: inf. n. of علق 'to hang, to be suspended,' &c.
def: s.m. Hanging, suspended; adhering (to); — anything hung or suspended; a suspensory; — attachment, love, affection.

ety: for A. علاقة, inf. n. of علق 'to be attached,' &c.
def: s.m. Attachment, connection, dependence, relation, affinity; concern, interest; part; reference, bearing (to), relevancy; commerce (with), intercourse, correspondence, communication; — a depenpendency, a province, division (of territory), district, parish; a tenure, holding; an estate, a manor; jurisdiction; a business, concern; an office, post
subs: s.m. A gold-lace maker; a braider, a maker of fringe, tape, &c.
s.f. Gold-lace work; lace-work; braiding; the making of fringe, or tape, &c.; — the business of lace-work, &c.
adj. & s.m. Connected, dependent, related; — a connection, a relation; a dependant; — the person who becomes responsible for the payment of the rates, &c. of a district or village, &c.

(-se), To be connected (with), to be dependent (on), to be related (to); to have to do (with), to have concern or interest (in), to concern, interest; to belong (to); to relate or refer (to), to have regard (to)
Beyond the province (of), beyond the jurisdiction, or the limits (of)
(-ke), Within the limits (of), within the jurisdiction (of).

ety: ʻalāqa, q.v.+ī = Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Attachment, connection, relation, dependence, &c. (see next).
adj. & s.m. Unseen; invisible; without shape or form (as the Deity); — a form of salutation among Jogīs
subs: lit. 'To call on the invisible'; to ask alms in the name of God
alakh-nāmī, s.m. A mendicant who acknowledges no deity but Brahma (see āzād)
adj. & s.m. Penetrating the unseen; — an epithet of God.

r: alaḵẖ, aliḵẖ, ila āḵẖirihi, Et cætera
def: adv. Et cætera, and so on.
adj. & s.m. = alakshaṇ, q.v.
s.f. pl. (of القية alqīyat), Questions, problems: — misfortunes.
adj. & s.m. = alakh, q.v.
s.m. Bad sign, ill omen; mark of ill fortune; — adj. Without distinguishing marks; inauspicious.
adj. & s.m. Unfortunate, ill-fated; — an unlucky wight, ill-fated person.
part. adj. Unseen; invisible; undistinguishable.
adj. Not prosperous, unfortunate, poor; — s.f. Ill-luck, adverse fortune.
adj. & s.m. = alaṛ, q.v.
subs: s.m. A frisky colt or calf, a young colt; — an inconsiderate or thoughtless person, a giddy person, one who thinks only of enjoyment and pleasure

(see ṭapnā, 'to jump'), adj. & s.m. Thoughtless; unsteady, unstable; giddy, frivolous; volatile, fickle; irregular, improper, absurd; — a thoughtless, giddy person, &c.; — adv. Thoughtlessly; at random, at a venture, by guess
(see jhap), adj. Unsteady, wavering, fickle, &c. ( = alal-ṭappū); untrustworthy; negligent, careless.

def: v.n. = allānā, q.v.

ety: prob. corr. fr. عليلة, fem. of ʻalīl, q.v.
def: s.f. Ailment, malady, infirmity ( = ʻillat).

ety: cf. alal, alaṛ and Ved. S. alalā
def: s.m. Feasting, festivity, pleasure, revelry, rioting, sensual enjoyment; show, pomp, ostentation; extravagance, waste, dissipation
subs: v.n. To feast, revel, riot; to give oneself up to pleasure, enjoy oneself; to lead a voluptuous and dissolute life; — to dissipate, squander, waste.

def: s.m. Support, &c. = ālamb, q.v.; sticks set up for the support of creeping plants and shrubs.
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit अलम् (álam).

def: s.m. Pain, anguish, torment; grief, affliction
subs: adj. Full of pain, agonizing; full of sorrow.
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit अलम् (álam).

def: adj. Adequate to, equal to, able; sufficient, enough, abundant.
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit अलम् (álam).

def: s.m. The world, the universe; men, people, creatures; regions; kingdom (in comp., e.g. 'vegetable-kingdom'); — age, period, time, season; state, condition, case, circumstances; a state of beauty; a beautiful sight or scene
subs: s.m. The world above, the heavens, heaven; the inhabitants of the heavens
s.m. Asylum of the world; a king; your majesty, &c. ( = jahān-panāh)
s.m. The lower world, the earth
s.m. The visible or external world
s.m. The invisible world, the world of spirits, the future state, the next world
s.m. The perishable world, the present world
adj. & s.m. World-traversing; — a great traveller
adj. & s.m. Conquering the universe, world-conquering; world-embracing, world-wide, universal, general; — conqueror of the world; a title of Aurangzeb
s.m. A state of intoxication, or of lustfulness
s.m. The invisible or spiritual world
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عَالَم (ālam), borrowed from Arabic عَالَم (ʕālam), borrowed from Aramaic עָלְמָא or Classical Syriac ܥܳܠܡܳܐ.

def: s.m. A mark, sign, token, &c. ( = ʻalāmat, q.v.); figured, or embroidered, work (on a cloth or garment); a decoration; — a spear; a flag (or strip of cloth, that is tied upon the spear); a banner, standard; the spear-headed hanner of Ḥasan and Ḥusain (that is carried in procession at the Moḥarram festival); — (in Gram.) a proper name or noun
wety: Borrowed from Arabic عِلْم (ʕilm).

def: The title and the opening words of Sūra 94 of the Qorʼān), adj. Lit. "have we not opened" (thy breast); — manifest, clear, evident, well-known.
s.m. pl. (of الم alam), Pains, griefs, cares, misfortunes.
adj. compar. and superl. (of عالم ʻālim), Wiser; wisest; very wise.

s.m. = P علامت ʻalāmat, s.f. (for A. علامة, v.n. fr. علم 'to know'), A mark, sign, token, an indication, a symptom; an index, exponent; a characteristic; a cognizance, a badge, device, emblem, a coat of arms; — a flag, standard (syn. ʻalam).
v.s.v. al. — (For all compounds beginning with the article al, see under that word.)

s.f. = P علامه ʻalāma, s.m. (for A. علامة, v.n. fr. علم 'to know'), A mark, sign, token, an indication, a symptom; an index, exponent; a characteristic; a cognizance, a badge, device, emblem, a coat of arms; — a flag, standard (syn. ʻalam).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عَلَامَت (alāmat), borrowed from Arabic عَلَامَة (ʕalāma). First attested in c. 1609 as Middle Hindi علامت ('lamt /⁠'alāmat⁠/).

def: s.f. pl. of ʻalāmat, q.v.
s.m. Hanging down; a perpendicular; support, prop; protection; asylum; aid, help.
s.m. Depending on, resting upon; support, maintenance, dependence; supporter, protector.
(rel. n. fr. ʻālam), adj. of the world, worldly, mundane, existing in the world.
s.m. Worlds; created beings or things.
s.m. pl. (of ʻālamī), People of this world, men, mankind, mortals.
s.m. Straw, &c. mixed with mud (for brick-making and building); a mixture of dāl and dough.
s.m. The post to which an elephant is tied; the rope or chain which binds him; fetter, tie; the brushwood used for creepers to run on.
s.m. The post to which an elephant is tied; the rope or chain which binds him; fetter, tie; the brushwood used for creepers to run on.

s.f. Line, row; border, edge, side, end; way, direction; (in South-western India), a line or row of buildings or shops, any long building (as a barrack, &c.: cf. next); postposn. By the side (of, -kī), along, towards
s.f. Longing, desire (for the male), heat, salaciousness, lustfulness
subs: To get into, or to be in, heat (a mare, &c.); to be lustful (a woman).

def: s.f. Line of circumvallation, lines of defence, intrenchment, trenches ( = morćâl); wall of a town.
s.f. = alganī, q.v.

ety: for A. علانية ʻalāniyat, inf. n. of علن 'to become open, or public,' &c.
def: adj. & adv. Open, manifest, public; — openly, publicly, aloud ( = A. ʻalāniyataṉ).
s.m. Ornament, decoration, embellishment; trinket, jewel; ornate composition; figure of speech, rhetoric.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit अलंकार (alaṃkāra).

def: part. Ornamented, decorated, adorned, embellished.
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit अलंकृत (alaṃkṛta).

def: s.m. A bonfire; a fire kindled in a hole in the ground, round which the villagers sit and warm themselves, and, in parts of India, around which Moḥammadans dance in the festival of Moḥarram.
s.f. Serious accident, unforeseen misfortune, sudden and violent death.

s.m. Association, intercourse; speaking, conversation, discourse; enumeration of the questions in an arithmetical or algebraic sum; — s.f. Modulation, or raising of the voice in singing, tuning up; prelude to a song
subs: s.f. Tuning the voice preparatory to singing.

def: s.m. Association, intercourse; speaking, conversation, discourse; enumeration of the questions in an arithmetical or algebraic sum; — s.f. Modulation, or raising of the voice in singing, tuning up; prelude to a song
subs: s.f. Tuning the voice preparatory to singing.

def: s.m. Leaves of trees.
